title: AWF Execution Contract
description: Canonical input boundary contract for AWF governance loop. Produced by Phase 0 Input Contract Adapter from raw natural-language requests, structured JSON files, A2A messages, or PM spec artifacts.

execution_contract.description: The typed work-item contract consumed by the AWF governance loop.

clarified_spec.description: Concise, single-line task description derived from the raw input. Not a full spec — AWF ingests, it does not brainstorm.
examples: Fix false positive in threshold check

scope_boundary.description: Explicit in/out scope for the task. When input is raw natural language, both lists default to empty.

in_scope.description: Files, modules, or areas that are within scope for this task.

out_of_scope.description: Files, modules, or areas explicitly excluded from this task.

success_criteria.description: Measurable, testable criteria that define when the task is complete. When input is raw natural language, this list defaults to empty — humans must fill it in during contract review.

validation_plan.description: Step-by-step validation steps to confirm the task meets success criteria. Generic entry ('Run full test suite') is added for obvious fix/bug inputs; otherwise defaults to empty for raw input.

risk_level.description: Risk assessment for the task. Determines governance guards and HITL mode.

recommended_layer.description: AWF routing layer recommendation derived from keyword scan of raw input. L4_release tasks must be delegated to agent-releaser.

next_step.description: Human-readable description of the immediate next action to take, based on the recommended layer.

residual_ambiguity.description: List of unresolved questions or ambiguities that remain after ingestion. Always includes a note for raw natural-language input: 'Input was raw — contract needs human review for scope and criteria'. Humans must address these before the task enters execution.
examples: Input was raw — contract needs human review for scope and criteria
